text: World record and national record running speeds scale logarithmically with race times, derivable from basic principles of metabolic power supply.
A minimal power model using relative metabolic power scale can predict race performances from 800m to marathon with mean errors of less than 1%.
The same logarithmic speed-time model applies to world records, national records, and individual personal bests across distances from 800m to marathon.
A characteristic time scale comparable to the duration a runner can sustain VO2max separates short racing events from long racing events and defines a symmetry in endurance performance.

Personalized characteristic race speeds for different durations and distances can be derived from an individual runner's performances using a self-consistency relation for time-dependent maximal power output.
